column2: xOrder — createOrderFromCart Idempotency (Duplicate Call)
column7: Runner-native. Full checkout + createOrderFromCart + second createOrderFromCart on same cartId. Expects second call to fail gracefully (not create duplicate order). CREATES A REAL ORDER.
column10:
[ERRORS label=create_first] errors[] empty
[DATA label=create_first] data.createOrderFromCart.id is non-null
column11: [EVIDENCE] create_second: either (a) errors[] non-empty with cart-already-converted message OR (b) returns the same order id as create_first (idempotent). Either is acceptable (BL-ORD-005). No duplicate order with different id.
column12: Two orders created with different ids (duplicate inventory decrement — P0); HTTP 500; silent success
column13: Creates real order — Admin cleanup if needed

column2: xOrder — Second createOrderFromCart Returns Graceful Response
column7: Runner-native. Covered pragmatically by CRX-GQL-024 — the second createOrderFromCart assertion. This case adds explicit graceful-error verification for the second attempt on a converted cart.
column10:
[ERRORS label=create_first] errors[] empty
[DATA label=create_first] data.createOrderFromCart.id is non-null
[ERRORS label=create_second] errors[] non-empty
column11: [EVIDENCE] create_second: errors[] non-empty with cart-already-converted message. NO HTTP 500, no stack trace, no duplicate order. Silent success on second create (errors[] empty + new order id) is a P0 bug — duplicate inventory decrement.
column12: HTTP 500; unhandled exception; stack trace in errors[]; duplicate order created (errors[] empty + new id); silent no-op (errors[] empty + data null) which would mask the lifecycle violation
column14: xAPI; BL-ORD-005 graceful handling; Pinned to stable @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id) — fixes false-FAIL from product search non-determinism. See BUG-xAPI-addItem-Silent-NoOp-On-Product-Availability-Gate.md. (2026-05-16)

column2: xCart — Cart Invalidated After Order Creation
column7: Runner-native. Create order, then attempt to addItem to the same (now converted) cartId → expect error or no-op (no items added).
column10:
[ERRORS label=create_order] errors[] empty
[DATA label=create_order] data.createOrderFromCart.id is non-null
[COUNT label=add_after_convert] data.addItem.itemsCount <= 1
column11: [EVIDENCE] add_after_convert: cart must NOT grow beyond the pre-conversion state (itemsCount<=1 — the original item remains, no new item is appended). Either errors[] non-empty rejecting the addItem OR itemsCount stays at 1 (silent no-op). NO HTTP 500. Silent acceptance (itemsCount=2) is a P0 bug — duplicate inventory decrement after order.
column12: Converted cart silently accepts new addItem with itemsCount > 1 (P0 — duplicate inventory decrement after order); HTTP 500
column14: xAPI; BL-CROSS-010 cart lifecycle; Pinned to stable @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id) — fixes false-FAIL from product search non-determinism. See BUG-xAPI-addItem-Silent-NoOp-On-Product-Availability-Gate.md. (2026-05-16)

column2: xOrder — createOrderFromCart with Empty Cart
column7: Runner-native. Clears cart then attempts createOrderFromCart on empty cart. Expects errors[] non-empty with empty-cart message.
column9:
[AUTH role=ORG_USER]
[GQL-OP setup_me]
  query { me { id } }
[GQL-EXEC setup_me]
[GQL-CAPTURE setup_me.data.me.id → USER_ID]
[GQL-OP pre_clear]
  mutation { clearCart(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" }) { id } }
[GQL-EXEC pre_clear]
[GQL-CAPTURE pre_clear.data.clearCart.id → CART_ID]
[GQL-OP create_empty]
  mutation { createOrderFromCart(command: { cartId: "{{CART_ID}}" }) { id number status } }
[GQL-EXEC create_empty]
column10:
[ERRORS label=create_empty] errors[] non-empty
[DATA label=create_empty] data.createOrderFromCart is null
column11: [EVIDENCE] create_empty: errors[] non-empty with empty-cart/validation message AND data.createOrderFromCart is null. NO HTTP 500 or server crash. Order must NOT be created. Silent no-op (200 + null data + errors[] empty) is a P0 bug — fail the test.
column12: Order created with 0 items (P0: empty order); HTTP 500; stack trace in errors[].message; silent no-op (errors[] empty + data null) which would mask the validation failure

column2: changeCartConfiguredItem Against Non-Configured LineItem — Clean Rejection (VCST-4960)
column7: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Cross-domain validation: add a regular (non-configurable) product to cart via @td(CFG_HOODIE.id) (confirmed non-configurable alias), capture its lineItemId, then call changeCartConfiguredItem referencing that lineItemId with any configurationSections payload. Verifies the validation is not limited to configurable products — the mutation must reject cleanly (validationErrors[] non-empty) without HTTP 500 or data corruption.
column10:
[ERRORS label=add_plain] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_plain] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=change_on_plain] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=change_on_plain] data.changeCartConfiguredItem.validationErrors.length >= 1
column11: [EVIDENCE] Non-configurable lineItem has no catalog configurationSections — any sectionId will fail catalog lookup. Mutation must reject gracefully. Record actual errorCode vs the configurable-product variant (may differ). NOTE: verify CFG_HOODIE alias resolves to a non-configurable product (was originally tagged for hoodie variant tests but suitable here).
column12: HTTP 500 on non-configurable target (unhandled null product config); errors[] non-empty at transport level (GraphQL exception escaped); configurationItems populated unexpectedly on a plain item
column14: xAPI; Mutation.changeCartConfiguredItem; VCST-4960; Cross-domain validation (050b4 scope); LIVE 2026-05-21: FAILED — validationErrors.length=0 (expected >=1), mutation silently no-ops on non-configurable lineItems instead of rejecting. Kept Draft until BL contract is confirmed via product owner.

column2: [VCST-5083] addItem × 5 Rapid Sequential Calls — itemsCount Equals N (Lock Stress)
column7: Runner-native. ORG_USER. VCST-5083 regression: pre-fix NoLockService caused lost-update under concurrent addItem. Simulates race window via 5 rapid sequential addItem calls for same productId (qty=1 each). Post-fix expectation: itemsQuantity=5, itemsCount=1 (BL-CART-001 merge rule).
column10:
[ERRORS label=add_5] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_5] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.itemsQuantity = 5
column11: [EVIDENCE] BL-CART-001: addItem accumulates quantity on same productId. itemsCount stays 1; itemsQuantity reaches 5.
column12: itemsQuantity < 5 (lost-update — pre-fix lock bug); itemsCount > 1 (duplicate lines)
column14: VCST-5083; BL-CART-001; xAPI addItem lock regression; XCart 3.1013.0 fix; LIVE 2026-05-21: FAILED — read_back.cart query returns itemsCount=0 / itemsQuantity=0 instead of expected 1/5. Looks like cart() query is reading a different cart than the add_1..5 wrote to (likely cartName mismatch — addItem may default-name carts that cart() can't find without cartName arg). Kept Draft until query cart-name parameterization is fixed.

column2: [VCST-5083] removeCartItem Immediately After addItem — Cart Reaches Empty
column7: Runner-native. ORG_USER. VCST-5083 race window: addItem → removeCartItem with captured lineItemId. Post-fix: cart empty (itemsCount=0). Pre-fix: stale-read race could no-op the remove.
column9:
[AUTH role=ORG_USER]
[GQL-OP setup_me]
  query { me { id } }
[GQL-EXEC setup_me]
[GQL-CAPTURE setup_me.data.me.id → USER_ID]
[GQL-OP cleanup_pre]
  mutation { clearCart(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" }) { id itemsCount } }
[GQL-EXEC cleanup_pre]
[GQL-OP add_item]
  mutation { addItem(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" productId: "@td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id)" quantity: 1 }) { id itemsCount items { id productId sku quantity } } }
[GQL-EXEC add_item]
[GQL-CAPTURE add_item.data.addItem.items.0.id → LINE_ITEM_ID]
[GQL-OP remove_item]
  mutation { removeCartItem(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" lineItemId: "{{LINE_ITEM_ID}}" }) { id itemsCount items { id productId quantity } } }
[GQL-EXEC remove_item]
[GQL-OP read_back]
  query { cart(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" currencyCode: "USD" cultureName: "en-US") { id itemsCount items { id productId } } }
[GQL-EXEC read_back]
[GQL-OP cleanup_post]
  mutation { clearCart(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" }) { id } }
[GQL-EXEC cleanup_post]
column10:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=remove_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=remove_item] data.removeCartItem.itemsCount = 0
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.itemsCount = 0
column11: [EVIDENCE] BL-CART-008: removeCartItem fully removes the line just created. Pre-fix: remove could no-op due to stale read.
column12: remove_item.itemsCount = 1 (remove no-op'd — pre-fix lock bug); read_back.itemsCount = 1
column14: VCST-5083; BL-CART-008; xAPI removeCartItem lock regression; XCart 3.1013.0 fix
